seoTitle: Cheero Point vs Cheltenham — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Cheero Point and Cheltenham. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Cheero Point vs Cheltenham: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cheero Point and Cheltenham.
heading: Cheero Point vs Cheltenham — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cheero Point, New South Wales and Cheltenham, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cheero Point has a lower overall climate risk score (42/100) compared to Cheltenham (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cheero Point or Cheltenham safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cheero Point has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Cheltenham scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cheero Point scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Cheltenham scores 58/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cheero Point or Cheltenham?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cheero Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
